title: Digitaria extensa
taxonomy: Digitaria extensa is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Digitaria, which consists of over 300 species of grasses. Digitaria extensa is further classified as a member of the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. The species is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. Its classification is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies, which confirm its placement within the Digitaria genus.
benefits: Digitaria extensa, also known as hairy crabgrass, has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Its extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain cancer cells and to reduce oxidative stress. Additionally, Digitaria extensa has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Digitaria extensa. More studies are required to confirm its effectiveness in treating specific health conditions.

constituents: Digitaria extensa contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. intro: Digitaria extensa, also known as large crabgrass, is a type of annual grass native to Africa and Asia, but now widely distributed across the globe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Digitaria extensa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in the form of dried leaves and roots for various health remedies.
